title: Managers and proprietors in agriculture and horticulture
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the management or operation of farms, ranches, greenhouses, aquacultural operations, nurseries, timber tracts, or other agricultural establishments. May hire, train, and supervise farm workers or contract for services to carry out the day-to-day activities of the managed operation. May engage in or supervise planting, cultivating, harvesting, and financial and marketing activities. Design chemical plant equipment and devise processes for manufacturing chemicals and products, such as gasoline, synthetic rubber, plastics, detergents, cement, paper, and pulp, by applying principles and technology of chemistry, physics, and engineering. Directly supervise and coordinate the activities of agricultural, forestry, aquacultural, and related workers. (Based on 4 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Analyze soil to determine types or quantities of fertilizer required for maximum crop production.
Manage nurseries that grow horticultural plants for sale to trade or retail customers, displays or research.
Direct and monitor trapping, spawning, egg incubation, fry rearing, and transfer of mature fish, applying knowledge of management and fish culturing techniques.
Determine how to allocate resources to respond to unanticipated problems such as insect infestation, drought, and fire.
Determine plant growing conditions and set planting and care schedules.
Prepare reports required by state and federal laws and maintain financial, operational, production, or employment records for farms or ranches.
Coordinate activities such as clerical, record-keeping, inventory, requisitioning, and marketing.
Direct the breeding and raising of stock, such as cattle, poultry, or honeybees, using recognized breeding practices to ensure stock improvement.
Negotiate with buyers for sale, storage, or shipment of crops or livestock.
Provide information to customers on the care of trees, shrubs, flowers, plants, and lawns.
Analyze market conditions to determine acreage allocations and determine types or quantities of crops, plants, or livestock to be grown based on budgets and projected sales volumes.
Supervise the construction of farm or ranch structures and monitor construction projects such as horticultural buildings or irrigation systems.
Conduct inspections to determine crop maturity, condition, or to detect disease or insect infestation, and inspect facilities for maintenance needs.
Confer with managers to determine production requirements, evaluate soil conditions, and develop plans or procedures.
Prepare and maintain time or payroll reports, as well as details of personnel actions such as performance evaluations, hires, and promotions.
Calculate or monitor budgets for maintenance or development of collections, grounds, or infrastructure.
Perform both supervisory and management functions, including accounting, marketing, and personnel work.
Transport or arrange for transport of animals, equipment, food, and other supplies to and from work sites.
Treat animal illnesses or injuries, following instructions of veterinarians.
Schedule work crews, equipment, or transportation for multiple work locations.
Train workers in techniques such as planting, harvesting, weeding, and use of safety measures, as well as in spawning and rearing methods and equipment usage.
Monitor workers to ensure adherence to safety regulations, warning or disciplining those who violate regulations, and assign tasks related to animal feeding and maintenance.
Record the numbers and types of fish or shellfish reared, harvested, released, sold, and shipped.
